pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to modernize point-to-point integrations and adopt a more governed architecture. Which approach is more aligned with MuleSoft?
opcoes: Implement API-led connectivity with System, Process, and Experience layers
justificativa: API-led connectivity is the governed alternative to point-to-point integration. Breaking capabilities into System, Process, and Experience layers improves reuse, governance, and long-term maintainability.
correta_texto: Implement API-led connectivity with System, Process, and Experience layers

pergunta: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) needs to access SAP data in a reusable way for multiple projects. What type of API should be created first?
opcoes: System API to expose SAP data
justificativa: In API-led connectivity, a System API abstracts the underlying system of record and provides a stable, reusable interface. Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) needs to access SAP data in a reusable way for multiple projects. This reduces coupling and keeps back-end changes from leaking into multiple consumers.
correta_texto: System API to expose SAP data

pergunta: A company needs to combine customer data (CRM) and inventory data (ERP) in a 'Create Order' process. Which layer does that normally belong to?
justificativa: A Process API is the correct place to combine data and business logic from multiple systems. It orchestrates System APIs and prepares reusable business capabilities for different consumers.
correta_texto: Process API

pergunta: A mobile app needs a specific and simplified payload to display requests to an end-user. Which layer should expose this interface?
justificativa: An Experience API is meant to shape data for a specific consumer or channel, keeping consumer-specific needs out of lower layers. A mobile app needs a specific and simplified payload to display requests to an end-user.
correta_texto: Experience API

pergunta: In a test scenario, one option suggests placing all data transformation directly into System APIs. Why is this usually incorrect?
opcoes: Business transformations and orchestration mainly belong in Process APIs
Experience APIs cannot consume System APIs
justificativa: System APIs should mainly abstract and expose the underlying systems. Reusable business transformations and orchestration usually belong in Process APIs, where they can serve multiple consumers consistently.
correta_texto: Business transformations and orchestration mainly belong in Process APIs

pergunta: The test presents a cycle: Design → Simulate → Implementation → Deploy → Manage. What platform does that describe?
opcoes: Anypoint Platform (API Designer, Mocking Service, Studio, Runtime Manager, API Manager)
justificativa: Anypoint Studio is appropriate when the integration needs custom logic, complex transformations (DataWeave), advanced error handling, or high control over runtime behavior. The test presents a cycle: Design → Simulate → Implementation → Deploy → Manage.
correta_texto: Anypoint Platform (API Designer, Mocking Service, Studio, Runtime Manager, API Manager)

pergunta: NTO wants Flow and RPA teams to start working before current API implementation. Which resource is more relevant?
justificativa: Anypoint Exchange is the catalog for discovering, publishing, and reusing assets (APIs, templates, connectors, examples) across teams. NTO wants Flow and RPA teams to start working before current API implementation. Using Exchange supports standardization and reduces duplicated work.
correta_texto: Mocking Service of Anypoint Exchange

pergunta: One exam question talks about unlocking data from central systems and isolating destination complexity. Which layer is being described?
justificativa: That description matches a System API because it abstracts the underlying system of record and shields consumers from the complexity of the destination system.
correta_texto: System API

pergunta: A company publishes an API specification in Exchange so Salesforce Flow can consume it via External Services. What asset is being published?
justificativa: To expose a contract that Flow can consume through External Services, the relevant asset is the API specification itself. Publishing the RAML or OAS definition makes the interface discoverable and reusable.
correta_texto: API Specification (RAML/OAS)

pergunta: In one scenario, multiple teams want to consume the same API of customers for different use cases. Which approach is more efficient?
opcoes: Create an identical new API for each team
Expose a reusable System API and, when necessary, Experience specific APIs
Restrict API uses to a single team
justificativa: In API-led connectivity, a System API abstracts the underlying system of record and provides a stable, reusable interface. In one scenario, multiple teams want to consume the same API of customers for different use cases. This reduces coupling and keeps back-end changes from leaking into multiple consumers.
correta_texto: Expose a reusable System API and, when necessary, Experience specific APIs

pergunta: AnyAirlines needs access control, rate limiting and central API monitoring. Which component is primarily responsible for this?
justificativa: API Manager is used to apply governance to APIs through policies, SLAs/tiers, and analytics/visibility. AnyAirlines needs access control, rate limiting and central API monitoring. This is the correct layer for enforcing controls consistently, without changing each implementation.
correta_texto: API Manager

pergunta: One option says that 'Experience APIs must directly access databases without System APIs to reduce hops.' How should this be evaluated?
opcoes: Wrong; this violates API-led separation of responsibilities
justificativa: Experience APIs should shape data for a specific consumer, not bypass the lower layers and talk directly to databases. Skipping System APIs breaks API-led separation of concerns and reduces reuse and governance.
correta_texto: Wrong; this violates API-led separation of responsibilities

pergunta: A team wants to edit API contracts, get feedback and simulate calls before implementing code. What tool is used in the Design phase?
justificativa: API Designer is correct because the design phase is where teams define the API contract first, review it with stakeholders, and simulate requests before implementation starts. That supports a design-first approach and helps catch misunderstandings before any Mule code is built.
correta_texto: API Designer

pergunta: NTO needs to choose the right layer to expose data to a customer service application in Salesforce without exposing internal system details. What's the best option?
justificativa: When Salesforce needs a consumer-friendly interface without exposing internal system details, the best fit is an Experience API designed for that channel.
correta_texto: Salesforce-specific Experience API

pergunta: One option says that 'API-led Connectivity is just a name for any set of APIs without clear rules.' What is the correct reading?
opcoes: False; API-Led clearly defines System, Process, and Experience API roles focusing on reuse and governance
justificativa: That statement is false because API-led connectivity defines clear roles for System, Process, and Experience APIs. Its value comes from governance, reuse, and deliberate separation of responsibilities.
correta_texto: False; API-Led clearly defines System, Process, and Experience API roles focusing on reuse and governance

pergunta: AnyAirlines is defining the API development process. What order correctly represents the life cycle within the Anypoint Platform?
opcoes: Design → Simulate → Feedback → Implementation → Deploy → Manage
justificativa: The Anypoint lifecycle starts with design and simulation, incorporates feedback before implementation, and then moves through deployment and ongoing management. That sequence reflects how governed API delivery should evolve.
correta_texto: Design → Simulate → Feedback → Implementation → Deploy → Manage

pergunta: NTO wants to allow consumers to explore APIs, see documentation and test calls on an interactive console. Which component should be used?
justificativa: Anypoint Exchange is the catalog for discovering, publishing, and reusing assets (APIs, templates, connectors, examples) across teams. NTO wants to allow consumers to explore APIs, see documentation and test calls on an interactive console. Using Exchange supports standardization and reduces duplicated work.
correta_texto: Anypoint Exchange (API Portal)

pergunta: A team needs to edit Mule flows, add connectors, and write DataWeave. Which tool is used?
justificativa: Anypoint Studio is appropriate when the integration needs custom logic, complex transformations (DataWeave), advanced error handling, or high control over runtime behavior. A team needs to edit streams Mule, add connectors and write DataWeave.
correta_texto: Anypoint Studio

pergunta: A company needs to scale the number of workers for an application as API usage grows. Where is this configured?
justificativa: Runtime Manager is the correct place because worker size, replica count, and runtime scaling are operational settings of the deployed application. When API traffic grows, teams adjust capacity there to keep performance stable.
correta_texto: Runtime Manager

pergunta: In a scenario, the solution must run in the company's own data center because of strict compliance requirements. Which deployment option is more aligned?
opcoes: CloudHub with secure connectivity back to the data center
On-Premises or Runtime Fabric, according to context
Composer-based integration running from SaaS connectors
RPA bots hosted near the internal applications
justificativa: When compliance or network constraints require workloads to stay close to internal systems, on-premises deployment or Runtime Fabric is often the best fit.
correta_texto: On-Premises or Runtime Fabric, according to context

pergunta: A proof item describes a container and Kubernetes-based scenario for running Mule applications. Which MuleSoft technology is being referenced?
justificativa: Runtime Fabric is MuleSoft's option for running Mule applications on containerized infrastructure such as Kubernetes, giving teams more control over where workloads run.
correta_texto: Runtime Fabric (RTF)

pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to share an internal API with external partner developers, with access control and “Request Access” button. What resource supports this?
opcoes: Portal / Exchange API with Client ID/Secret Flow
justificativa: Anypoint Exchange is the catalog for discovering, publishing, and reusing assets (APIs, templates, connectors, examples) across teams. AnyAirlines wants to share an internal API with external partner developers, with access control and “Request Access”... Using Exchange supports standardization and reduces duplicated work.
correta_texto: Portal / Exchange API with Client ID/Secret Flow

pergunta: NTO has an API that will be consumed by Salesforce Flow via External Services and by RPA via HTTP. Which practice best supports this reuse?
opcoes: Publisher specification and API as Exchange assets
Create the separate API for each consumer
justificativa: Anypoint Exchange is the catalog for discovering, publishing, and reusing assets (APIs, templates, connectors, examples) across teams. NTO has an API that will be consumed by Salesforce Flow via External Services and by RPA via HTTP. Using Exchange supports standardization and reduces duplicated work.
correta_texto: Publisher specification and API as Exchange assets

pergunta: A team wants to simulate the credit API before definitive implementation, allowing Flows and RPA to use fictional data. What should be used?
justificativa: Anypoint Exchange is the catalog for discovering, publishing, and reusing assets (APIs, templates, connectors, examples) across teams. A team wants to simulate the credit API before definitive implementation, allowing Flows and RPA to use fictional data. Using Exchange supports standardization and reduces duplicated work.
correta_texto: Exchange Mocking Service

pergunta: One option says that the 'Manage' phase refers only to shutting down the API when necessary. What is the correct view?
opcoes: False; 'Manage' includes applying policies, controlling consumption, analytics, and governance
Depends on the type of API
It applies only to public APIs
justificativa: The Manage phase is about operating and governing APIs after deployment. That includes policies, access control, analytics, visibility, and ongoing lifecycle management.
correta_texto: False; 'Manage' includes applying policies, controlling consumption, analytics, and governance

pergunta: The company needs to see relationships between APIs and dependencies on a visual map. What capability does that refer to?
justificativa: Viewer is correct because it shows the relationship map between APIs and dependencies across the landscape. That helps teams understand impact, upstream and downstream connections, and where a change may affect other integrations.
correta_texto: Viewer

pergunta: NTO wants to ensure that changes in a shared RAML fragment do not break dependent projects without visibility. What Exchange concept addresses this?
justificativa: Dependency tracking in Exchange helps teams see where a shared asset is used, making it easier to understand the impact of changes before they cause downstream problems.
correta_texto: Dependency tracking

pergunta: A candidate suggests implementing APIs directly in API Manager. Why is that incorrect?
opcoes: API Manager is for governance and policies, not code development
justificativa: API Manager is for governance, policies, SLAs, and analytics, not for developing API implementations. The implementation work belongs in tools such as Anypoint Studio and the runtime layer.
correta_texto: API Manager is for governance and policies, not code development

pergunta: AnyAirlines needs to decide between CloudHub and On-Premises. The scenario emphasizes simplified access to internal systems without exposing firewalls. Which option can be more appropriate?
opcoes: CloudHub with firewall exceptions into the internal network
On-Premises or RTF close to internal systems
Composer with connectors reaching the internal systems directly
RPA running on shared desktops near the target systems
justificativa: When access to internal systems is the key concern, on-premises deployment or Runtime Fabric close to those systems is often the better architectural fit.
correta_texto: On-Premises or RTF close to internal systems

pergunta: One option says: 'For each new project, create a brand-new API with a different URL to make team management easier.' Why is this usually a bad idea?
opcoes: Because APIs cannot have different URLs
Because this reduces reuse and increases fragmentation, which goes against MuleSoft strategy
Because Exchange does not support multiple APIs
Because CloudHub does not support more than one application
justificativa: Creating a new API for every project fragments the landscape and reduces reuse. MuleSoft's strategy is to build reusable assets and evolve them thoughtfully instead of duplicating them by default.
correta_texto: Because this reduces reuse and increases fragmentation, which goes against MuleSoft strategy

pergunta: AnyAirlines notes that different teams are building direct integrations to the same legacy system without using common APIs. Which MuleSoft practice fights this problem the most?
opcoes: Create Reusable System/Process APIs and catalog them in Exchange
justificativa: A Process API centralizes business logic and orchestration across one or more System APIs, enabling reuse and consistent rules. AnyAirlines notes that different teams are building direct integrations to the same legacy system without using commo... This is the typical place for cross-system aggregation and business-level transformations.
correta_texto: Create Reusable System/Process APIs and catalog them in Exchange

pergunta: NTO wants to apply Rate Limiting policies to an API without changing Mule code. Which component should be used?
justificativa: API Manager is the right component because rate limiting is applied as a policy at the management layer, not inside the Mule implementation. That lets teams protect the API and control traffic without changing or redeploying the application code.
correta_texto: API Manager

pergunta: An exam scenario: a Flow is overloading a legacy system via API, causing crashes. What answer is more aligned?
opcoes: Scale the legacy system first and keep traffic patterns unchanged
Apply Rate Limiting or SLA-based Tiering in the API to protect the system
Reduce logging to relieve pressure on the system
Expose a second direct integration path that bypasses the API
justificativa: To protect downstream systems from spikes, you typically enforce throttling/rate limits via API management policies and scale capacity when needed. An exam scenario: a Flow is overloading a legacy system via API, causing crashes.
correta_texto: Apply Rate Limiting or SLA-based Tiering in the API to protect the system

pergunta: One option suggests exposing an API without any form of authentication to simplify consumption by RPA, Flow, and Composer. How should this be evaluated?
opcoes: Wrong; policies like Client ID Enforcement are recommended even in critical internal scenarios
justificativa: Internal APIs still need governance and access control. Policies such as Client ID Enforcement help protect shared services even when the consumers are internal tools like Flow, Composer, or RPA.
correta_texto: Wrong; policies like Client ID Enforcement are recommended even in critical internal scenarios

pergunta: A company wants to ensure that only Salesforce infrastructure IPs access a given API. What policy is used?
justificativa: If the requirement is to allow traffic only from known Salesforce infrastructure addresses, IP whitelisting is the policy that directly enforces that constraint.
correta_texto: IP Whitelisting

pergunta: In one scenario, several critical APIs are part of a hyperautomation flow. What combination helps you see availability and errors in real time?
opcoes: Anypoint Monitoring with dashboards and Log Search
justificativa: Anypoint Monitoring provides observability (dashboards, metrics, logs, and alerts) to detect issues and measure performance across runtimes and APIs. In one scenario, several critical APIs are part of a hyperautomation flow.
correta_texto: Anypoint Monitoring with dashboards and Log Search

pergunta: One item describes a “shock” system because one Flow Fired Thousands of simultaneous requests. The correct alternative probably talks about:
opcoes: Combine Rate Limiting and, if necessary, adjust capacity (vColors/workers)
justificativa: To protect downstream systems from spikes, you typically enforce throttling/rate limits via API management policies and scale capacity when needed. One item describes a “shock” system because one Flow Fired Thousands of simultaneous requests.
correta_texto: Combine Rate Limiting and, if necessary, adjust capacity (vColors/workers)

pergunta: NTO wants to monitor not only whether an API is up, but also whether it responds correctly to critical scenarios. What resource is being referenced?
justificativa: Functional Monitoring is used for synthetic checks that verify both availability and expected behavior. It is the right choice when the exam emphasizes validating real outcomes instead of basic uptime only.
correta_texto: Functional Monitoring

pergunta: One option says: 'Whenever a new consumer appears, create a new API just for that consumer, even if a generic API already exists.' Why is this usually wrong?
opcoes: Because APIs cannot have more than one consumer
Because it violates reuse and increases coupling, going against the API-led model
Because no API can be generic
Because API Manager does not support multiple policies
justificativa: Duplicating APIs for every new consumer weakens reuse and creates unnecessary coupling. API-led architecture encourages deliberate reuse and consumer-specific shaping only where it really belongs.
correta_texto: Because it violates reuse and increases coupling, going against the API-led model

pergunta: In one scenario, an Experience API makes heavy transformations and multiple system aggregations. There is already a Process API that does the same. Which option is more aligned?
opcoes: Keep Double Logic in the Experience API
Centralize transformation in Process API and maintain Finer experience
justificativa: A Process API centralizes business logic and orchestration across one or more System APIs, enabling reuse and consistent rules. In one scenario, an Experience API makes heavy transformations and multiple system aggregations. This is the typical place for cross-system aggregation and business-level transformations.
correta_texto: Centralize transformation in Process API and maintain Finer experience

pergunta: A team argues that there is no need to publish assets in Exchange because everyone already knows where the code is. Why is that architecturally weak?
opcoes: Because without a central catalog, reuse, discovery, and governance become much harder
Because APIs do not work without Exchange
Because Runtime Manager depends on Exchange
justificativa: Without a central catalog like Exchange, teams lose discoverability and make reuse much harder. That increases duplication and weakens governance over time.
correta_texto: Because without a central catalog, reuse, discovery, and governance become much harder

pergunta: AnyAirlines wants RPA, Flow, and Composer to use the same customer API. Which design is most consistent with MuleSoft strategy?
opcoes: Each tool calls a separate wrapper API owned by its team
All tools call the same governed API published in Anypoint Platform
Only Flow uses the governed API while the other tools integrate directly
Keep RPA and Composer on direct connectors to move faster
justificativa: The MuleSoft strategy favors exposing a governed API that can be reused by multiple consumers instead of building one-off integrations for each tool.
correta_texto: All tools call the same governed API published in Anypoint Platform

pergunta: An exam question mentions 'Shared Load Balancer' (SLB). What does it represent?
opcoes: The default load balancer that provides a public URL for APIs in CloudHub
An exclusive component of Runtime Fabric
justificativa: In CloudHub, the Shared Load Balancer is the platform-managed public entry point that exposes APIs when no dedicated load balancer is configured. In exam terms, it represents the default shared ingress layer rather than a custom component owned by one application.
correta_texto: The default load balancer that provides a public URL for APIs in CloudHub

pergunta: NTO wants to limit traffic to an API based on each consumer's access plan (e.g. Bronze, Silver, Gold). What functionality is more appropriate?
opcoes: A single global rate limit shared by all consumers
IP whitelisting with the same quota for every consumer
Manually disable the API during peak times
justificativa: SLA-based policies in API Manager let different consumers receive different traffic quotas such as Bronze, Silver, and Gold. This is the standard way to control access plans without changing the API implementation.
correta_texto: SLA-based policies in API Manager

pergunta: One option states: 'In a good MuleSoft architecture, it is better to duplicate APIs than adjust existing contracts to enable reuse.' How should the ideal candidate respond?
opcoes: Disagree; the strategy values reuse, well-designed contracts, and minimization of duplication
It depends on the team's mood
Accept the approach without questioning it
justificativa: The preferred answer is to disagree. MuleSoft architecture values reuse, stable contracts, and controlled evolution instead of duplicating APIs whenever requirements change.
correta_texto: Disagree; the strategy values reuse, well-designed contracts, and minimization of duplication
